meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve Project Stagnation
content.seo_description: Overcome project paralysis with this intermediate guide. Master Git workflows using Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade AI applications.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea. Use AI-assisted Git workflows to rapidly prototype, iterate, and version-control your concepts. By leveraging Claude Code for architectural scaffolding and Antigravity for automated repository structuring, you transform vague thoughts into professional, deployable codebases, effectively bypassing project paralysis and building a high-impact portfolio.
content.intro:
The blank editor window is the graveyard of ambition. You’ve mastered the syntax, passed the tutorials, and now you’re staring at a void, paralyzed by the paradox of choice. The difference between a student and a software engineer isn't just knowledge; it's the ability to translate messy, abstract requirements into a robust, version-controlled architecture.

We are shifting the paradigm. By integrating AI-driven agents directly into your Git workflow, you no longer build alone. You treat your codebase as a living organism, evolving through iterative prompts and automated versioning. This is how you stop 'learning' and start shipping.

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope with AI
steps.description: Use AI to narrow your project idea into a constrained, 48-hour buildable scope to ensure completion.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. I have a vague idea for a [Insert Idea]. Break this down into a 48-hour MVP scope with clear technical milestones and a database schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a project roadmap and file structure for a [Project Name] MVP'.

steps.title: Initialize Repository with AI-Generated Boilerplate
steps.description: Create a clean, standardized project structure that follows industry best practices immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a production-ready Next.js project with TypeScript, Tailwind, and Supabase integration. Include a structured README.

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Build features module by module, using Claude Code to ensure type safety and consistent logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement an authentication middleware using Supabase and NextAuth. Focus on edge-case error handling.

steps.title: Automated Version Control and Commit Hygiene
steps.description: Use AI to analyze your changes and generate professional, conventional-commit style documentation for every Git push.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze these changes and write a concise, professional commit message following conventional commits standards.

steps.title: Refactoring and Technical Debt Review
steps.description: Run an AI audit on your codebase to identify bottlenecks, security flaws, and performance optimizations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a code review on this repository. Identify security vulnerabilities and suggest refactoring for better performance.

pro_tips: Always commit your AI-generated architectural prompt to a '/docs/prompts' folder; it acts as a 'DNA sequence' for your project's intent.
Use Claude Code to automate your .gitignore creation based on your specific framework to avoid committing sensitive env files.
Treat your Git history as a story for future employers; use AI to summarize complex feature branches into clear, readable pull request descriptions.
When stuck, ask the AI to 'write a test case for this function' before writing the function itself—this is TDD for the modern era.
Set up a 'Context File' that summarizes your project state, and feed this into your AI agents at the start of every coding session to maintain coherence.
resources: Git Best Practices for AI Teams: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is functional, document your process in a blog post and open-source your repository on GitHub to showcase your workflow to recruiters.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting perfect be the enemy of done. Your first project is just the beginning of your engineering legacy. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
